qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection in P7M file processing via unsanitized exec() call
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable function (XML::decodeP7M at src/Util/XML.php:100), same injection sink (exec() with unsanitized file path from uploaded .p7m filename), same entry points (importFE_ZIP and importFE plugins), and same CWE-78 (OS Command Injection) as the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authorization bypass in file download endpoint permits unauthorized access to all system files
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability class (authorization bypass vs command injection), different vulnerable code (view.php vs XML.php decodeP7M), different root cause (missing permission check vs unsanitized exec input). No overlap with advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe deserialization in OAuth2 token handling permits attacker-controlled object injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability class (unsafe deserialization vs command injection), different vulnerable code (OAuth2.php vs XML.php decodeP7M), different root cause (unserialize without allowed_classes vs unsanitized exec input). Requires prior database access which is a separate precondition.

selected_finding.title: Command injection in P7M file processing via unsanitized exec() call

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The vulnerability_command_injection finding identifies the exact advisory-described vulnerable code (XML::decodeP7M at src/Util/XML.php:100) with the same root cause (unsanitized file path passed to exec()), same entry points (importFE_ZIP and importFE plugins), and same CWE-78. PoC execution confirmed arbitrary OS command execution: a malicious P7M filename containing double-quote and semicolon metacharacters broke out of the exec() quoting and executed injected commands (touch and id), satisfying the exploit success predicate.
evidence: Finding identifies same vulnerable function XML::decodeP7M() at src/Util/XML.php:100
Same injection sink: exec('openssl smime -verify -noverify -in "'.$file.'" -inform DER -out "'.$output_file.'"')
PoC confirmed command injection: filename ';touch PROOF_CMD_INJECTION;'.p7m with double-quote breakout executed 'touch PROOF_CMD_INJECTION' creating proof file
PoC confirmed id command execution: uid=503 output captured from injected 'id' command
Same CWE-78 (OS Command Injection) as advisory

poc_executions.command: python3 simulate_exec_with_malicious_filename (direct shell simulation, Docker build failed due to network timeout)
poc_executions.observed_effect: Created malicious P7M file with double-quote and semicolon metacharacters in filename. Simulated the vulnerable PHP exec() call from src/Util/XML.php:100. The injected 'touch PROOF_CMD_INJECTION' command was executed via semicolon injection, creating the proof file. Additionally, the 'id' command was injected and its output (uid=503) was captured, confirming arbitrary OS command execution through filename injection in the decodeP7M() function.
